skill.name: journal-recommender
skill.description: Recommend academic journals based on manuscript topic, abstract, and impact factor expectations. Use when the user wants to find suitable journals for their research manuscript, especially when they provide a topic, abstract, and target Impact Factor.
skill.summary: Analyzes manuscript content and recommends academic journals in Sprint, Robust, and Safe categories based on impact factor targets.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 41 static analysis findings are false positives. The 13 weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are triggered by non-cryptographic text in comments and non-code files. The 22 shell backtick execution alerts are Markdown fenced code blocks showing example bash commands, not executable backtick injections. The 3 system reconnaissance alerts are documentation best practices. The 3 hardcoded URL alerts reference legitimate PubMed and GitHub URLs. This skill is a legitimate academic journal recommender with no malicious intent. Risk factors (external_commands, network) exist only as documentation references and a PubMed URL template.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 13 instances of weak cryptographic algorithms. The actual code in scripts/journal_ranker.py performs only text parsing and sorting. No cryptographic operations (hashing, encryption, or signing) exist in the codebase. The alerts were triggered by non-cryptographic words in comments and JSON field labels.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 22 instances of shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. These are Markdown fenced code blocks (```bash ... ```) showing example commands like python scripts/journal_ranker.py --help. They are documentation examples, not executable shell injections.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 3 instances of system reconnaissance. These are documentation lines about output discipline and a return message for empty results. No reconnaissance or information gathering behavior exists in the code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 3 instances of hardcoded URLs. The PubMed URL at scripts/journal_ranker.py:47 is a legitimate academic database link template for formatting output entries. The GitHub URL at SKILL.md:7 is standard source attribution. No data exfiltration or malicious network behavior.

content.user_title: Find the Best Journals for Your Research Manuscript
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to identify the right publication venue for their work. This skill analyzes your manuscript topic, abstract, and impact factor expectations to recommend suitable academic journals in three categories.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes manuscript topic and abstract to determine research field and innovation level
Estimates manuscript impact factor potential based on content analysis
Categorizes journal recommendations into Sprint, Robust, and Safe tiers
Filters out journals from the CAS warning list
Provides structured output with journal names and impact factors per category
Sorts journal recommendations by impact factor in descending order
limitations: Does not have access to real-time journal databases or subscription-based journal lists
Cannot verify journal acceptance rates, review timelines, or current editorial policies
Impact factor estimates are based on content analysis, not on official publisher data
Does not support analysis of non-English manuscripts

use_cases.title: Target High-Impact Journal Selection
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher has completed a study and needs to find journals with an impact factor slightly above their target for ambitious submissions.

use_cases.title: Find Safe Publication Options
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs to discover journals with lower impact factors where their manuscript has a high chance of acceptance.

use_cases.title: Comprehensive Journal Matching
use_cases.description: An academic author wants a full publication strategy covering ambitious, matching, and safe journal options for their research paper.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a manuscript titled [title] with this abstract: [abstract]. My target impact factor is [number]. Please recommend suitable academic journals.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a completed manuscript

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Manuscript Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Please analyze my manuscript for journal matching. Topic: [topic]. Abstract: [abstract]. Target IF: [number]. Article type: [type]. Categorize recommendations into Sprint, Robust, and Safe tiers with at least 5 journals per category.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wanting thorough evaluation with full categorization

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Target Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: I am deciding between targeting high-impact or mid-range journals for my manuscript on [topic]. Compare journal options for target IF of [high value] versus [low value]. Include Sprint, Robust, and Safe categories for each scenario.
prompt_templates.scenario: User exploring different impact factor ranges

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow with Output File
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full journal recommender workflow for this manuscript. Topic: [topic]. Abstract: [abstract]. Target IF: [number]. Article type: [type]. Run the validation script, then produce recommendations with at least 5 journals per category. Save the output to journal_recommender_result.md and provide a validation summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wanting reproducible results with file output

output_examples.input: Topic: Machine learning for protein structure prediction. Abstract: We present a novel deep learning approach for predicting protein tertiary structures from amino acid sequences. Target IF: 8.0
output_examples.output: Sprint Journals (IF slightly above target): Nature Methods (47.0), Nature Biotechnology (45.5), Nature Machine Intelligence (25.0), PLOS Computational Biology (4.5), Bioinformatics (6.5). Robust Journals (IF matches target): Briefings in Bioinformatics (9.5), Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling (5.5). Safe Journals (IF below target): BMC Bioinformatics (3.0), Journal of Computational Biology (2.0), Proteins (2.5).

output_examples.input: Topic: Urban heat island effect mitigation. Abstract: This study analyzes the impact of green infrastructure on reducing urban heat island effects in Mediterranean cities. Target IF: 4.0
output_examples.output: Sprint Journals (IF +0 to +5 above target): Urban Climate (6.0), Landscape and Urban Planning (6.0), Building and Environment (7.0). Robust Journals (IF matches target): International Journal of Climatology (3.5), Theoretical and Applied Climatology (3.5). Safe Journals (IF below target): Sustainability (3.3), Atmosphere (2.5), Climate (2.0).

best_practices: Provide the full abstract rather than a short summary for more accurate journal matching.
Specify a realistic target impact factor based on your research field and study scope.
Review the CAS warning list for your field before submitting your manuscript to ensure compliance.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for journal recommendations without providing both a topic and abstract as these are required inputs.
Do not request journals from a single publisher only as this limits the recommendation pool.
Do not use the same target impact factor for all manuscripts without considering field-specific differences.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need to provide the manuscript topic, abstract, and your target Impact Factor. Optional inputs include the full manuscript text and article type.

faq.question: What are Sprint, Robust, and Safe journals?
faq.answer: Sprint journals have an IF slightly higher than your target (up to +5). Robust journals match your target IF. Safe journals have a lower IF and higher acceptance chance.

faq.question: Can this skill access real journal databases?
faq.answer: No. It provides recommendations based on content analysis of your manuscript and known journal information. It does not access subscription databases or real-time journal listings.

faq.question: How accurate is the impact factor estimation?
faq.answer: The estimation is based on analyzing your manuscript content, not on official publisher metrics. Use the results as a guide, not a definitive measure.

faq.question: What is the CAS warning list?
faq.answer: The CAS warning list contains journals flagged by the Chinese Academy of Sciences for questionable publishing practices. This skill will not recommend journals from this list.

faq.question: Can this skill handle non-English manuscripts?
faq.answer: No. The current version only supports analysis of English-language manuscripts.
